zh.lifeSummary: 當Jeanne de Meulan de Beaumont於大約1160年出生在法國奧維阿利也波蒙特時，她的父親Robert de Beaumont20歲，母親Maud de Dunstanville18歲。她於1179年與Robert II 'le Vaillant' d'Harcourt結婚。他們至少是7個兒子的父母。她去世於1186年享年27歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jeanne de Meulan de Beaumont于大约1160年出生在法国奧維阿利也波蒙特时，她的父亲Robert de Beaumont20岁，母亲Maud de Dunstanville18岁。她于1179年与Robert II 'le Vaillant' d'Harcourt结婚。他们至少是7个儿子的父母。她去世于1186年享年27岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Meulan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Galician (Merlán): habitational name from any of the seven places called Merlán in Galicia (Spain), from the ancient Germanic personal name Merila.
Romanian: nickname derived from mierlă ‘blackbird’.

nameMeanings.name: Beaumont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Gilles, Gaetan, Honore, Jacques, Jean-Guy, Marcel.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the five places in Normandy or several others elsewhere in France so named. The placename comes from Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + mont ‘hill’. There are also places in England so named under Norman influence in Cumberland, Lancashire, and Essex, the last of which changed its name in the 12th century from Fulepet ‘foul pit’ to Bealmont ‘beautiful hill’; these may also have given rise to the surname. The surname is now widespread throughout England, but most common in Yorkshire. Compare Belmont .

History: Many English bearers of this surname are descended from Henry de Beaumont (died 1340), who came to England from France as a soldier, serving Kings Edward I, II, and III of England. He acquired the earldom of Buchan in Scotland by marriage. — Many American bearers of this surname are descendants of John Beaumont (1612–47), who came to North America from England in 1630. William Beaumont came from England to Cambridge MA in 1635 and subsequently moved to CT.

variants.name: Bement
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): variant of   Beaumont  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beeman
meanings: English (Kent): occupational name for a beekeeper, from Middle English bee-man ‘bee-keeper’ (Old English bēo + mann).
English (Kent): variant of   Beaumont  .
Americanized form of German Biemann or of its Dutch cognate Bieman.

variants.name: Belmont
meanings: French and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beaumont  .
Catalan: habitational name from the placename Bellmont ‘beautiful mountain’. Compare Spanish   Belmonte  .

variants.name: Baumunk
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Siegmund.
German: probably a Germanized form of a French Huguenot name Beaumont (see also Beaumont ).

variants.name: Bayman
meanings: English (mainly Lancashire): variant of   Beaumont  .
Americanized form of German Beumann or Bäumann, variants of Baumann .

nameMeanings.name: Jeanne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Feminine form of   Jean .

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jeanne de Meulan de Beaumont was born about 1160, in Beaumont, Allier, Auvergne, France, her father, Robert de Beaumont, was 20 and her mother, Maud de Dunstanville, was 18. She married Robert II 'le Vaillant' d'Harcourt in 1179. They were the parents of at least 7 sons. She died in 1186, at the age of 27.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jeanne de Meulan de Beaumont est née environ 1160, à Beaumont, Allier, Auvergne, France, son père, Robert de Beaumont, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Maud de Dunstanville, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé Robert II 'le Vaillant' d'Harcourt en 1179. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils. Elle est décédée en 1186, à l’âge de 27 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jeanne de Meulan de Beaumont um 1160 in Beaumont, Allier, Auvergne, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert de Beaumont 20 und ihre Mutter Maud de Dunstanville 18. Sie hat 1179 Robert II 'le Vaillant' d'Harcourt geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne. Sie starb 1186 im Alter von 27 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jeanne de Meulan de Beaumont è nata il circa 1160 nel Beaumont, Allier, Auvergne, Francia, sua padre, Robert de Beaumont, aveva 20 anni e sua madre, Maud de Dunstanville, aveva 18 anni. Lei sposato Robert II 'le Vaillant' d'Harcourt nel 1179. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli. Morì nel 1186, all'età di 27 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jeanne de Meulan de Beaumontが約1160年にフランスアウバーグンアリールビーウモントで生まれた時，父Robert de Beaumontは20歳，母Maud de Dunstanvilleは18歳であった。1179年にRobert II 'le Vaillant' d'Harcourtと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子の親であった。彼女は1186年に27歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jeanne de Meulan de Beaumont(이)가 대략 1160년 프랑스 오버그니 앨리어 보몽에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert de Beaumont(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud de Dunstanville(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 1179년 Robert II 'le Vaillant' d'Harcourt(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1186년 27세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jeanne de Meulan de Beaumont nasceu aproximadamente 1160, em Beaumont, Allier, Auvergne, França, seu pai, Robert de Beaumont, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Maud de Dunstanville, tinha 18 anos. Ela casou-se com Robert II 'le Vaillant' d'Harcourt em 1179. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1186, com 27 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jeanne de Meulan de Beaumont родилась около 1160 г. в местности бомонт, аллиир, аувиргн, Франция, её отцу по имени Robert de Beaumont было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud de Dunstanville, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert II 'le Vaillant' d'Harcourt в 1179 г. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей. Она умерла в 1186 г. в возрасте 27 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jeanne de Meulan de Beaumont nació aproximadamente 1160, en Beaumont, Allier, Auvergne, Francia, su papá, Robert de Beaumont, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Maud de Dunstanville, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Robert II 'le Vaillant' d'Harcourt en 1179. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos. Murió en 1186, a la edad de 27 años.
